reasoning: The Bank Term Funding Program (BTFP) ceased making new loans on March 11, 2024. As these loans were for a maximum term of one year, all outstanding loans matured by March 2025, and the FRED data confirms the balance of the facility fell to precisely 0.0 at the end of March 2025. It has remained at 0.0 through October 26, 2025. For the value to increase, the Federal Reserve would have to experience another banking crisis, legally authorize the reopening of this specific emergency facility under Section 13(3), and banks would need to request loans under it. Given the extreme rarity of such crises and the likelihood that a new facility (with a new name) would be created for any future crisis rather than reopening a closed 2023 facility, the probability of an increase is exceptionally low. Given the short horizon for the resolution dates (1 week, 1 month, 3 months), the likelihood remains at the absolute minimum floor of 2%. I have slightly increased the probability to 3% for the farthest date (Jan 2026) to reflect very marginally increased background uncertainty.
